title: Oracle SCM Techno-Functional Consultant
description:
Role Overview

We are seeking an experienced Oracle SCM Techno-Functional Consultant to support and deliver enterprise Oracle ERP initiatives across implementation, enhancement, and production support. This role requires a strong balance of functional business process expertise and hands-on technical capability, working closely with business stakeholders, IT teams, and external implementation partners.

The consultant will play a key role in ensuring robust solution delivery during project phases while maintaining operational stability and continuous improvement post go-live.

Key Responsibilities

Solution Delivery & Implementation

  • Support end-to-end Oracle ERP delivery covering requirements analysis, solution design, build, testing, deployment, and post-implementation support
  • Work with business users to document current and target-state processes, functional designs, and configuration artefacts
  • Collaborate with implementation partners and internal teams to ensure solutions align with business objectives and Oracle best practices

Functional & Technical Enablement

  • Configure and support Oracle Supply Chain and Distribution modules across enterprise environments
  • Design and support integrations, reports, workflows, and conversion programs
  • Execute data migration, validation, and reconciliation activities using Oracle-recommended tools and patterns
  • Support custom extensions, reporting, and system enhancements where required

Testing, Training & Operational Support

  • Define testing approaches, prepare test scenarios and scripts, and execute unit and system integration testing
  • Support UAT activities, manage issue resolution, and obtain business sign-off
  • Deliver user training and provide post-go-live application support
  • Analyze and resolve complex issues related to system data, configuration, and business processes

Change & Stakeholder Management

  • Track operational issues and coordinate resolution across project and BAU landscapes
  • Perform change impact analysis for quarterly releases and enhancements
  • Act as a liaison between business users, vendors, and IT teams to ensure timely and quality delivery

Requirements

Experience & Background

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related discipline
  • 5 to 7+ years of Oracle ERP experience with multiple end-to-end implementations
  • Strong functional knowledge of Order-to-Cash processes, with exposure to Procure-to-Pay in global environments
  • Hands-on experience supporting Oracle EBS R12 and Oracle Fusion Cloud

Functional Expertise

  • Oracle Fusion SCM modules include Purchasing, Sourcing, Supplier Portal, Inventory, Warehouse Management, Pricing, and Order Management
  • Exposure to Global Trade Management or RF-based warehouse solutions is an advantage

Technical Expertise

  • Experience with Oracle Fusion data models, integrations, and reporting
  • Hands-on skills in BI Publisher, OTBI dashboards, PL/SQL, XML Publisher, OAF, ADF, and personalization
  • Experience with Oracle Cloud data conversion approaches such as FBDI, FBDL, and ADFDi
  • Understanding of Oracle PaaS and SaaS architecture
  • Prior exposure to Oracle EBS to Fusion Cloud migration is a strong plus

Additional Skills

  • Comfortable working directly with end users in enterprise environments
  • Familiarity with Agile or SCRUM delivery methodologies
  • Strong communication skills with high attention to detail
  • Self-driven, collaborative, and adaptable to cross-cultural team dynamics
  • Ability to engage Mandarin-speaking users to gather and clarify business requirements

Please send your detailed resume in MS Word format to resume@goldtecHRs.com with

  • Education Level
  • Working experiences
  • Each employment background
  • Reason for leaving each employment
  • Last drawn salary
  • Expected salary
  • Date of availability

We represent clients from a wide sector of industries both local and multi-national corporation.
